title: Malacothamnus niveus
intro: Malacothamnus niveus, also known as the white bush mallow or white flaxweed, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States and Baja California. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as the ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Malacothamnus niveus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Malacothamnus niveus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Malacothamnus niveus is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It is native to the western United States and is part of the genus Malacothamnus. This genus is considered a distinct group within the family Malvaceae. The classification of Malacothamnus niveus is further broken down into its kingdom, phylum, class, order, family, genus, and species levels. Malacothamnus niveus is classified as kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Malvales, family Malvaceae, genus Malacothamnus, and species M. niveus.
